name: Japanese Mountains 01
description: Important geography, culture, risks, and natural facts about Japanese mountains
author: rewouh

items.title: percentage of Japan covered by mountains
items.mnemonic: They are pretty unlucky, they got earthquakes, tsunamis, tropical storms and nearly three quarters of the country is mountainous! How did they make it?

items.title: height of Mount Fuji
items.mnemonic: Fuji stands at 3-7-7-6. Imagine climbing stairs labeled 3 → 7 → 7 → 6 leading directly to the highest viewpoint in Japan. Yup, four steps is all it takes.

items.title: second highest mountain in Japan
items.mnemonic: Fuji is first. Kita is jealous so he eats kitkats.

items.title: is Mount Fuji active or extinct
items.mnemonic: Fuji is sleeping, not dead. Imagine the mountain with one eye open while Tokyo nervously watches from afar.

items.title: what are the three holy mountains of Japan ?
items.mnemonic: Forming the Holy Trio : Fuji Haku Tate (same letters!).

items.title: percentage of the world's active volcanoes located in Japan
items.mnemonic: Picture all the volcanoes in the world erupting at the same time, Japan would be in way more trouble than everyone with 10% of all volcanoes!
